title: Agent OS ROI review: capability command and tool registry
goal: Capture the ROI case for a richer capability, command, and tool registry.
Keep the finding scoped to discovery and routing rather than external plugin productization.
scope: Issue #1115 ROI point 6: capability, command, and tool registry.
Commands, skills, adapters, proof surfaces, inputs, outputs, and automation safety.
non_goals: Do not define external plugin support in this review.
Do not change skill registries or command declarations.

review_mode.review question: Would a composed capability registry make command/tool selection cheaper and safer for agents?
review_mode.inputs inspected first: Issue #1115 body, startup skills projection, docs/agent-os-capabilities.md capability/module registry section

review_method.commands used: uv run agentic-workspace start --task "Create one planning review artifact for each of the ten agent OS capability ROI points from issue #1115" --format json
review_method.evidence sources: GitHub #1115; startup skills.catalog counts and command; docs/agent-os-capabilities.md

findings.title: Capability registry value is broader than module availability
findings.summary: Startup already reports available skill registries and counts, but an agent still has to infer what commands, adapters, proof surfaces, inputs, outputs, and safety constraints apply to a task.
findings.evidence: The startup payload exposed a skill catalog with 33 skills, source counts, and a command for task-specific skill search. Issue #1115 ranks richer command/tool discovery as high ROI and asks for inputs, outputs, and automation safety to be visible.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents may miss a relevant first-party command, use a generic shell path where a managed command exists, or overuse tools without understanding their safety and output contracts.
findings.suggested action: Create a child slice that composes module-owned command/skill/adaptor declarations into a task-routable capability registry, initially for first-party surfaces only.
findings.promotion target: GitHub child issue under #1115; Capability / Module Registry with Workspace Composition
findings.promotion trigger: Promote when task-specific discovery remains expensive despite skills output, or when command-generation extraction needs a shared declaration surface.
findings.post-remediation note shape: stub pointing to registry contract and discovery command output

recommendation.promote: High-value but probably after task/proof/ownership basics unless command discovery becomes the active bottleneck.
recommendation.defer: Keep as review evidence until a concrete registry consumer is selected.
recommendation.dismiss: Do not dismiss; the existing skills projection demonstrates a partial version of the need.

retention.trigger: A child issue or registry contract owns capability discovery scope.
retention.proof surface: Workspace skills/modules output and registry validation.

validation_commands: uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
drift_log: 2026-05-24: Review record created from issue #1115 ROI point 6.
